scope.selection_note: Codex-only deterministic prep from current data/canonical/projects.jsonl plus current identity merge and do-not-merge guards. Proposals are project-only, live in current canonical JSONL, not already project merge aliases, not target chains, and avoid broad program-vs-expansion, amendment-vs-program, and same-name different phase/stage traps.

proposed_aliases.reason: Both current records describe the same progressive design-build state-of-good-repair project for structural repairs across 43 NYCT stations in Manhattan and Brooklyn. The shared project name is exact, and both payload descriptions identify the same Phase 1 design/design-build scope.

validator_evidence.duplicate_issue: Global project records project_43-stations-sgr-phase1 and project_43-stations-sogr-design-build-oct2023 share identity key project_progressive-design-build-services-for-state-of-good-repair-work-at-various-stations.

alias.display_name: State of Good Repair Work at Various Stations

payload_fields.project_name: Progressive Design-Build Services for State of Good Repair Work at Various Stations

target.display_name: 43 NYCT Stations State of Good Repair - Design-Build

payload_fields.project_name: Progressive Design-Build Services for State of Good Repair Work at Various Stations

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records have the same project_name, completed status, and matching description of Grand Central circulation links among the 4/5/6, 7, Times Square Shuttle, One Vanderbilt transit hall, and Grand Central Madison.

validator_evidence.duplicate_issue: Global project records project_grand-central-circulation-improvements and project_grand-central-circulation-201601 share identity key project_grand-central-circulation-improvements.

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records describe the same Hartsdale Station ADA/accessibility improvement project: two elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks, and new curbing for the inbound and outbound station sides in Hartsdale/Town of Greenburgh.

validator_evidence.duplicate_issue: Global project records project_hartsdale-station-ada-improvements and project_hartsdale-station-improvements share identity key project_hartsdale-station-ada-improvements.

alias.display_name: Metro-North is investing in infrastructure improvements at the Hartsdale Station to support ADA accessibility and enhance pedestrian flow. The Project includes 2 new elevators, mechanical rooms, expanded sidewalks and new curbing, for both the inbound and outbound sides of the station with connections to the existing pedestrian overpass.

payload_fields.location: Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York
Hartsdale, Town of Greenburgh, New York

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records identify NYC Transit's Faster, Cleaner, Safer strategic/action plan. The target has the compact stable id; the alias record names the same plan with the expanded 'and Safer Strategic Action Plan' title.

validator_evidence.duplicate_issue: Global project records project_meeting-doc-104781-faster-cleaner-safer and project_faster-cleaner-safer-plan share identity key project_faster-cleaner-safer-plan.

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records identify the same MTA Fuel Hedging/Fuel Hedge Program for ULSD and compressed natural gas budget-risk mitigation. The target already aggregates multiple fuel hedge source records.

validator_evidence.duplicate_issue: Global project records project_mta-fuel-hedging-program and project_fuel-hedge-program-2022 share identity key project_fuel-hedging-program.

applied_to_all_proposals: alias_id and target_id are present in current data/canonical/projects.jsonl
alias_id is not already present as a project alias in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json
target_id is not a project alias in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json, preventing target chains
pair is not listed in current data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json project pairs
proposal has at least one shared validator identity key from identityKeysForRecord
selection avoids current stale aliases already removed by identity150/identity152 and avoids broad same-name-but-different-stage traps

explicitly_skipped_examples.reason: Current project do-not-merge pair; parent program versus child busway project.
Broad ABLE program versus expansion/lifecycle scope; not selected for this safe batch.
Capital program amendment versus base capital program; phase/amendment trap.
Shared generic '2017 Street Improvement Project' label but different street/location scopes.

command_evidence.command: bun -e 'import { validateRepo } from "./packages/pipeline/src/validate.ts"; const r=validateRepo(); const counts={}; for (const i of r.issues) counts[i.code]=(counts[i.code]||0)+1; console.log(JSON.stringify({duplicate_global_identity:counts.duplicate_global_identity||0, project_duplicate_global_identity:r.issues.filter(i=>i.code==="duplicate_global_identity" && i.message.includes("Global project records")).length}, null, 2));'
bun -e deterministic duplicate scan over data/canonical/projects.jsonl using identityKeysForRecord plus current merges/do-not-merge guards
bun -e guard pass for the 11 proposed alias/target pairs
bun -e selected-record source inspection from current data/canonical/projects.jsonl
command_evidence.output_summary: Inspected display_name, record_aliases, source_ids, payload project_name/project_type/status/description/location, and leading evidence_refs for each selected alias and target.
